The speed and deceleration rate of the smooth scroll when [`smooth_scroll_mode`](configs.md#smooth_scroll_mode) is enabled.
config_title_to_documentation_map.sort_bookmarks_by_location-sort_highligh:
# sort_bookmarks_by_location, sort_highlights_by_location
Sort bookmarks/highlights by location (instead of creation date).
config_title_to_documentation_map.startup_commands:
# startup_commands
`macro` commands to run then sioyek starts up. For example:

```
startup_commands toggle_dark_mode;toggle_scrollbar;set_status_string(hi there)
```

Will set the color scheme to dark mode and enable scrollbar and show a message in sioyek statusbar when sioyek starts up.
config_title_to_documentation_map.statusbar:
# statusbar
If set, we draw a statusbar at the bottom of the window.
config_title_to_documentation_map.statusbar_handles_wheel_events:
# statusbar_handles_wheel_events
If set, the statusbar will handle wheel events when the mouse is over it. For example, if the mouse cursor is over the page number in the statusbar and the user scrolls the mouse wheel, the page will change.
config_title_to_documentation_map.status_bar_color-status_bar_text_color:
# status_bar_color, status_bar_text_color
Text and background colors of statusbar.
config_title_to_documentation_map.status_bar_font_size:
# status_bar_font_size
Font size of the status bar.
config_title_to_documentation_map.status_bar_format-right_status_bar_forma:
# status_bar_format, right_status_bar_format, status_*_command
The items and format to show in the statusbar. The default value is:

```
[ %{current_page} / %{num_pages} ]%{chapter_name}%{search_results}%{search_progress}
%{link_status}%{waiting_for_symbol}%{indexing}%{preview_index}%{synctex}%{drag}
%{presentation}%{visual_scroll}%{locked_scroll}%{highlight}%{freehand_drawing}
%{rect_select}%{custom_message}%{download}%{download_button}%{network_status}
%{tts_status}%{tts_rate}
```


The [`right_status_bar_format`](configs.md#status_bar_format-right_status_bar_format-status_*_command) is similar, but determines what to display on right-aligned statusbar.

Here is what each of the `%{}` variables expand to:

- `%{current_page}` expands to the current page number
- `%{current_page_label}` expands to the the label of the current page (might be different from `current_page` for example some documents have pages labeled with roman numerals, e.g. XII)
- `%{num_pages}` expands to the total number of pages in current document
- `%{chapter_name}` expands to the name of current chapter
- `%{document_name}` expands to the file name of current document
- `%{auto_name}` expands to automatically detected document name
- `%{search_results}` expands to the search results (if there is a search active)
- `%{search_progress}` expands to the current search progress (if there is one in progress)
- `%{link_status}` specifies whether we are linking (that is the source of a [`portal`](commands.md#portal) has been set and we are waiting for the destination)
- `%{waiting_for_symbol}` specifies if a command is expecting a symbol to be entered
- `%{indexing}` specifies if the current document is currently being indexed
- `%{preview_index}` if there are more than one possible overviews available for the overview window, this option shows how many there are and what is the current index 
- `%{synctex}` displays if we are in synctex mode ([`toggle_synctex`](commands.md#toggle_synctex-turn_on_synctex))
- `%{drag}` displays if we are in mouse drag mode ([`toggle_mouse_drag_mode`](commands.md#toggle_mouse_drag_mode))
- `%{presentation}` displays if we are in presentation mode ([`toggle_presentation_mode`](commands.md#toggle_presentation_mode-turn_on_presentation_mode))
- `%{visual_scroll}` displays if we are in ruler scorll mode ([`toggle_ruler_scroll_mode`](commands.md#toggle_ruler_scroll_mode-toggle_visual_scroll))
- `%{locked_scroll}` displays if we have locked horizontal scrolling ([`toggle_horizontal_scroll_lock`](commands.md#toggle_horizontal_scroll_lock))
- `%{highlight}` displays information about the current highlight state ([`set_select_highlight_type`](commands.md#set_select_highlight_type)) 
- `%{freehand_drawing}` displays if we are in freehand drawing mode ([`toggle_freehand_drawing_mode`](commands.md#toggle_freehand_drawing_mode))
- `%{rect_select}` displays if a command requires a rectangle to be selected
- `%{point_select}` displays if a command requires a point to be selected
- `%{custom_message}` user message that can be set using [`set_status_string`](commands.md#set_status_string-clear_status_string)
- `%{download}` displays if we are currently downloading a paper
- `%{download_button}` a button that displays when the current overview is a reference to a paper, clickin on this button downloads the paper
- `%{closest_bookmark}` displays the closest bookmark to current location
- `%{closest_portal}` displays the closest portal to the current location
- `%{selected_highlight}` displays the comment of the selected highlight
- `%{mode_string}` displays the current mode string (this can be useful when defining `macro`s)
- `%{network_status}` displays the current network status
- `%{tts_status}` displays the current text to speech status
- `%{tts_rate}` displays the current text to speech speed
- `%{custom_message_a}` to `%{custom_message_d}` expands to a custom message which can be set using @config(status_custom_message_[a-d])

Note that clicking on many of these items performs a relevant command. For example clicking on the text expanded from `${current_page}` shows a page-selection widget or clicking on `%{chapter_name}` opens the table of contents. The exact command executed when some part of the statusbar is clicked can be configured using @config(status_*_command) config options where `*` can be any of the options described above. For example if we have the following in the `prefs_user.config` file:

```
status_num_pages_command toggle_dark_mode
```

then clicking on the number of pages in the statusbar toggles dark mode.
config_title_to_documentation_map.status_custom_message_[a-d]-status_custo:
# status_custom_message_[a-d], status_custom_message_[a-d]_command
Show custom messages which can be shown in statusbar if `%{custom_message_a}` to `%{custom_message_d}` is added to [`status_bar_format`](configs.md#status_bar_format-right_status_bar_format-status_*_command). When the message is clicked, the command configured in @config(status_custom_message_[a-d]_command) will be run.
config_title_to_documentation_map.status_font:
# status_font
Font face to use for menus.
config_title_to_documentation_map.strike_line_width:
# strike_line_width
The strike width when adding strike highlights (highlights with type `_`).
config_title_to_documentation_map.super_fast_search:
# super_fast_search
If set, we index the document so that searches can be performed instantaneously. Some other sioyek features work only if this option is enabled so I recommend to keep it enabled (the extra memory requirement due to this option being enabled is extremely small).
config_title_to_documentation_map.synctex_highlight_color:
# synctex_highlight_color
Highlight color to use when highlighting synctex search results. This only applies when [`use_ruler_to_highlight_synctex_line`](configs.md#use_ruler_to_highlight_synctex_line) is set to 0, otherwise we use the ruler to highlight the line.
config_title_to_documentation_map.synctex_highlight_timeout:
# synctex_highlight_timeout
Duration (in seconds) to highlight the synctex line. If the duration is -1, the highlight will stay indefinitely.
config_title_to_documentation_map.table_extract_behaviour:
# table_extract_behaviour
What to do with extracted table. The possible values are:

- `bookmark`: Create a freetext bookmark with the extracted table
- `copy`: Copy the extracted table into clipboard
config_title_to_documentation_map.tag_font_face:
# tag_font_face
Font face to use for keyboard tags.
config_title_to_documentation_map.text_highlight_color:
# text_highlight_color
The highlight color of selected text (e.g. when user selects text using mouse or using commands like [`keyboard_select`](commands.md#keyboard_select)).
config_title_to_documentation_map.titlebar_format:
# titlebar_format
Format of sioyek titlebar. The syntax and available options are the same as [`status_bar_format`](configs.md#status_bar_format-right_status_bar_format-status_*_command).
config_title_to_documentation_map.toc_jump_align_top:
# toc_jump_align_top
When jumping to a table of contents entry, align the top of the screen with the target location (instead of center of the screen).
config_title_to_documentation_map.touchpad_sensitivity:
# touchpad_sensitivity
Relative scrolling sensitivity when using touchpad or mouse wheel, this is a multiplier that modifies [`vertical_move_amount`](configs.md#vertical_move_amount-horizontal_move_amount) so the overal move amount when using touchpad or mouse wheel is `touchpad_sensitivity * vertical_move_amount`.
config_title_to_documentation_map.touch_mode:
# touch_mode
This config is suitable for touch devices, if set, many behaviours of sioyek change to better support touch devices. For exmaple dragging on the screen moves the document instead of selecting text and menus are now optimized for touch devices.
config_title_to_documentation_map.back_rect_tap_command-back_rect_hold_com:
# back_rect_tap_command, back_rect_hold_command, forward_rect_tap_command, forward_rect_hold_command, middle_left_rect_tap_command, middle_left_rect_hold_command, middle_right_rect_tap_command, middle_right_rect_hold_command, ruler_next_tap_command, ruler_next_hold_command, ruler_prev_tap_command, ruler_prev_hold_command, top_center_tap_command, top_center_hold_command
`macro` commands to run when the corresponding rectangle is tapped or held (only works in touch mode). You can view the location of these rectangles by executing executing [`toggle_rect_hints`](commands.md#toggle_rect_hints).
config_title_to_documentation_map.tts_rate:
# tts_rate
The text to speech speaking speed when using text to speech commands.
config_title_to_documentation_map.tts_rate_increment:
# tts_rate_increment
How much to increase/decrease the rate of the text-to-speech (TTS) engine by when using the [`increase_tts_rate`](commands.md#increase_tts_rate-decrease_tts_rate) and [`decrease_tts_rate`](commands.md#increase_tts_rate-decrease_tts_rate) commands.
config_title_to_documentation_map.tts_voice:
# tts_voice
The voice to use for text-to-speech when reading text. Possible values are system-dependent, you can scroll through them by running `setconfig_tts_voice` and pressing up and down arrows to scroll through the list.
config_title_to_documentation_map.ui_font:
# ui_font
Font face to use for menus.
config_title_to_documentation_map.portrait_back_ui_rect-portrait_forward_u:
# portrait_back_ui_rect, portrait_forward_ui_rect, landscape_back_ui_rect, landscape_forward_ui_rect, portrait_visual_mark_next, portrait_visual_mark_prev, landscape_visual_mark_next, landscape_visual_mark_prev, landscape_middle_right_rect, portrait_middle_right_rect, landscape_middle_left_rect, portrait_middle_left_rect
Configures the rectangles in touch mode. If the user taps on these rectangles, we execute the corresponding command as configured in [`touch_rect_commands`](configs.md#back_rect_tap_command-back_rect_hold_command-forward_rect_tap_command-forward_rect_hold_command-middle_left_rect_tap_command-middle_left_rect_hold_command-middle_right_rect_tap_command-middle_right_rect_hold_command-ruler_next_tap_command-ruler_next_hold_command-ruler_prev_tap_command-ruler_prev_hold_command-top_center_tap_command-top_center_hold_command).
config_title_to_documentation_map.ui_text_color-ui_background_color-ui_sel:
# ui_text_color, ui_background_color, ui_selected_text_color, ui_selected_background_color
Text and background colors of ui menus.
config_title_to_documentation_map.unselected_search_highlight_color:
# unselected_search_highlight_color
The highlight color to highlight unselected search matches when [`should_highlight_unselected_search`](configs.md#should_highlight_unselected_search) is set.
config_title_to_documentation_map.use_legacy_keybinds:
# use_legacy_keybinds
Use old-style keybinds when parsing key config files. This is kept for compatiblity with old config files and should be disabled for new configs.
config_title_to_documentation_map.use_ruler_to_highlight_synctex_line:
# use_ruler_to_highlight_synctex_line
Use the ruler to highlight the synctex search result (instead of highlighting it with a color).
config_title_to_documentation_map.use_system_theme-use_custom_color_as_dar:
# use_system_theme, use_custom_color_as_dark_system_theme
Try to use system theme for light/dark modes (might not work in some operating systems).
If [`use_custom_color_as_dark_system_theme`](configs.md#use_system_theme-use_custom_color_as_dark_system_theme) is set, then we use the custom color mode instead of dark mode when system theme is set to dark.
config_title_to_documentation_map.vertical_move_amount-horizontal_move_amo:
# vertical_move_amount, horizontal_move_amount
The amount to move when executing movement commands (including mouse wheel).
config_title_to_documentation_map.volume_up_command-volume_down_command:
# volume_up_command, volume_down_command
`macro` commands to run when the volume up or down key is pressed on mobile devices.
config_title_to_documentation_map.wheel_zoom_on_cursor:
# wheel_zoom_on_cursor
When performing zoom using control+mouse wheel, zoom on the mouse cursor instead of the center of the screen.
config_title_to_documentation_map.main_window_size-helper_window_size-main:
# main_window_size, helper_window_size, main_window_move, helper_window_move, single_main_window_size, single_main_window_move, overview_size, overview_offset
Default size and offset of sioyek windows. the `single_*` is used when only the main sioyek window is visible (no helper window) otherwise we use the other values.
config_title_to_documentation_map.zoom_inc_factor:
# zoom_inc_factor
The amount to multiply/divide the zoom level when executing zoom commands. For example, if the current zoom level is 2 and `zoom_inc_factor` is 1.5, then after executing [`zoom_in`](commands.md#zoom_in-zoom_out-zoom_in_cursor-zoom_out_cursor), the zoom level will be `2 * 1.5 = 3`.
